expectations.text: Uses a View component as the root container with explicit width and height
expectations.evidence: Line 18: `<View style={{ width: OUTPUT_WIDTH, height: OUTPUT_HEIGHT }}>` where OUTPUT_WIDTH=1920 and OUTPUT_HEIGHT=1080.

expectations.text: Uses InputStream component to display the video stream
expectations.evidence: Line 21: `<InputStream inputId="live_stream" />` wrapped in a Rescaler for proper sizing.

expectations.text: Uses Text component from @swmansion/smelter for the lower-third text
expectations.evidence: Lines 38-47 and 48-57 use `<Text>` components for "Jane Doe" and "Senior Software Engineer". Imported from `@swmansion/smelter` on line 2.

expectations.text: The text overlay is positioned at the bottom of the screen using absolute positioning (top/left/bottom/right props on a child View)
expectations.evidence: Lines 27-28: the child View uses `bottom: 80, left: 100` in its style, positioning the lower-third bar near the bottom-left of the screen.

expectations.text: Includes a backgroundColor with alpha channel for semi-transparency (e.g. #00000080 or similar)
expectations.evidence: Line 31: `backgroundColor: "#000000BB"` -- black with BB (73%) alpha, providing semi-transparency.

expectations.text: Text component has fontSize set (fontSize is required in TextStyleProps)
expectations.evidence: Line 40: `fontSize: 36` on the name Text, and line 50: `fontSize: 24` on the title Text. Both Text components have fontSize set.

expectations.text: The code shows the correct import pattern from @swmansion/smelter
expectations.evidence: Line 1: `import Smelter from "@swmansion/smelter-node"` for the runtime, and line 2: `import { View, Text, InputStream, Rescaler } from "@swmansion/smelter"` for the components. This matches the documented pattern of separating runtime from component imports.

claims.claim: InputStream does not have a style prop and must be wrapped in Rescaler or placed inside a View for sizing
claims.evidence: Transcript Step 2 states this and the code correctly wraps InputStream in Rescaler (line 20-22). The reference file InputStream.md was read to confirm this.
Transcript Step 2 and Step 5 confirm the #RRGGBBAA format from ViewStyleProps docs. Code uses #000000BB which follows this format.

claims.claim: Node.js lifecycle is new Smelter() -> init() -> registerInput() -> registerOutput() -> start()
claims.evidence: Code lines 64-88 follow exactly this sequence. Transcript Step 3 documents reading nodejs.md to confirm this.

claims.claim: All props were cross-checked against reference docs
claims.evidence: Transcript Step 5 enumerates each prop used and confirms validity. Metrics show 8 Read calls covering all relevant component and prop reference files.

claims.claim: Text width: 652 is calculated as View width (700) minus horizontal padding (24*2=48)
claims.evidence: 700 - 48 = 652. The math checks out. Noted in user_notes.md as a design decision.

uncertainties: RTMP server input type is experimental with no stream key validation or RTMPS support
Text width values (652) are manually calculated from container width minus padding and must be adjusted if dimensions change
Default font is Verdana; custom fonts require smelter.registerFont()
Rescaler defaults to fit mode which may letterbox if input aspect ratio differs from 1920x1080
needs_review: Whether rtmp_server is appropriate for production use or if whip_server/rtp_stream would be better
Whether rescaleMode should be 'fill' instead of default 'fit' depending on use case
workarounds: Use nginx-rtmp-module proxy for production RTMP ingestion
Set rescaleMode: 'fill' on Rescaler style to avoid letterboxing (at cost of cropping)

suggestions: Consider adding an expectation that verifies the Rescaler wrapper around InputStream, since this is a common mistake (applying style directly to InputStream).
Consider adding an expectation for the full Node.js lifecycle (init/registerInput/registerOutput/start) since that is a key part of a working Smelter app.
The semi-transparency expectation could be more specific about the format (#RRGGBBAA vs #RRGGBB with separate opacity) since Smelter uses a specific format.
Could add a negative expectation: 'Does NOT apply a style prop directly to InputStream' to catch a common API misuse.
eval_feedback.overall: All 7 expectations passed. The answer is a complete, well-structured TSX file that correctly uses the Smelter API. The executor read all relevant reference docs (10 files) and cross-verified props. The user notes are thoughtful and flag real production concerns. The expectations cover the core requirements well but could be strengthened with Smelter-specific API correctness checks (e.g., Rescaler wrapping, lifecycle order).
